summary: All 3 bug fixes verified successfully. P0 (autocomplete images), P1 (minimum 5 buzz score), and P2 (Brown Bread +100) are all working correctly.
passed_tests: P0: Autocomplete search returns images for all suggestions (fallback to ui-avatars.com when Wikipedia has no thumbnail)
P0: Generic names like 'Tom' and 'John' use fallback avatar images correctly
P0: Short queries (<2 chars) return empty suggestions as expected
P1: Celebrity buzz scores are always >= 5 (tested with Ed Sheeran: 45.5, Adele: 20.5, Taylor Swift: 21.5)
P1: Unknown celebrity searches still return minimum 5 buzz score
P2: How It Works section shows '5. Brown Bread' with '+100 if celeb dies!'
P2: Points Methodology modal shows 'Brown Bread Bonus 💀' with '+100 bonus points'
P2: Backend /api/points-methodology returns Brown Bread Bonus with 100.0 points_per_unit
API health check: Root endpoint returns version info
API health check: Categories endpoint returns list
API health check: Stats endpoint returns player/celebrity counts
context_for_next_testing_agent: All 3 bug fixes from the review request have been verified: (1) Autocomplete images with fallback working, (2) Minimum 5 buzz score enforced, (3) Brown Bread Bonus shows +100 in both How It Works and Points Methodology modal. Previous iteration 5 had a profanity filter bug - that was not in scope for this test but should be verified separately.

P0_autocomplete_images.evidence: API returns images for all suggestions. Generic names use ui-avatars.com fallback. Verified via API tests and UI screenshot.
P0_autocomplete_images.code_location: Backend server.py line 218: final_image = image if image and len(image) > 0 else f'https://ui-avatars.com/api/?name={name.replace(' ', '+')}&size=64&background=FF0099&color=fff'

P1_minimum_buzz_score.evidence: All tested celebrities have buzz scores >= 5. Ed Sheeran: 45.5, Adele: 20.5, Taylor Swift: 21.5
P1_minimum_buzz_score.code_location: Backend server.py line 444: return round(max(5.0, min(score, 150.0)), 1)

P2_how_it_works_100.evidence: UI shows '5. Brown Bread' with '+100 if celeb dies!' in How It Works section
P2_how_it_works_100.code_location:
Frontend App.js line 249:

+100 if celeb dies!

P2_methodology_100.evidence: Points Methodology modal shows 'Brown Bread Bonus 💀' with '+100 bonus points'
